extra_model_fields.notes: Planned changes are scoped to clarify modeling choices, improve verifiability, and expand sensitivity checks. These are low-to-medium risk changes that should not materially change the code-generation approach but will increase reviewer confidence and resolve the semantic deduction.

Planned changes are scoped to clarify modeling choices, improve verifiability, and expand sensitivity checks. These are low-to-medium risk changes that should not materially change the code-generation approach but will increase reviewer confidence and resolve the semantic deduction.

planned_changes: Open force_control_tuning.xlsx in a parsing-capable environment and export: (a) a CSV of computed cells (per-record computed columns and scenario aggregations) and (b) a plain-text listing of the formulas used for key computed columns and scenario aggregations.
In the spreadsheet, add explicit per-row formula references or comments mapping each computed cell back to its input fields (R1..R4) so every numeric output is traceable to an embedded-packet input.

Extend +/-5% sensitivity analysis to stiffness_N_mm, damping, peak_force_N, and trial_valid. For each perturbation (both -5% and +5%), record per-row margin/classification and set an expert_review flag where classification flips occur. Summarize which input(s) cause flips.

Provide an alternate modeling of O3 as a risk reduction (percentage applied to total observed risk) and include a side-by-side comparison table showing outcomes for both interpretations (original margin-boost and alternate risk-reduction).

Add a worked example (row or scenario near decision boundary) in the markdown report showing step-by-step formula evaluation and intermediary values to demonstrate correctness of formulas and sensitivity.

Ensure all scenario combination computations (e.g., O1+O2, O1+O3) are implemented as spreadsheet formulas (not hard-coded). Update the report to reference the exact spreadsheet cells used for aggregations.
Add an explicit note recommending SME safety review of the proposed program-change instructions prior to deployment, referencing the sample recomputation and any records with expert_review flags.
raw_llm_output.revision_reason: Address verifier/grader non-blocking issues: provide verifiable formula outputs (XLSX CSV/formula export), extend sensitivity testing to all relevant inputs, and resolve or justify nonstandard O3 modeling so SMEs can evaluate recommendations without ambiguity.

raw_model_output.revision_reason: Address verifier/grader non-blocking issues: provide verifiable formula outputs (XLSX CSV/formula export), extend sensitivity testing to all relevant inputs, and resolve or justify nonstandard O3 modeling so SMEs can evaluate recommendations without ambiguity.

metadata_json.revision_goal: Artifacts are present and traceable; deterministic precheck and verifier confirm deliverable completeness. Semantic deductions (10%) were applied by the grader for two non-blocking issues: (1) O3 was modeled as a direct margin boost rather than a risk-reduction consistent with O1/O2, which is a nonstandard representation that materially affects results; (2) sensitivity analysis was limited to +/-5% on peak_force only rather than applying perturbations to all relevant inputs (stiffness_N_mm, damping, peak_force_N, trial_valid). Additionally, the verification environment could not open the XLSX preview (openpyxl unavailable), so direct, cell-level confirmation that numeric outputs are formula-derived could not be performed here. Provide the requested clarifications and workbook exports to resolve these non-blocking issues and strengthen the deliverable for SME review.
